judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are thorough and nearly equivalent across all five questions; Report 1 is slightly stronger on expectation (b) because it unambiguously states the STAGE-based gating itself is unsound (not just fragile) and proposes removing the environment-based bypass entirely, whereas Report 2 partially defends the STAGE check as "reasonable" before flagging its fragility.

report_1.evidence: NON_RETRYABLE = (BadRequestError, ContentPolicyError, AuthError) ... Only RateLimitError, RequestTimeoutError, ServerError (and any truly-unclassified exception... per project documentation's "everything else fails over" default) should trigger the fallback to secondary.

report_2.evidence: Retry-to-secondary only for RateLimitError, RequestTimeoutError, ServerError. Re-raise (or explicitly fail the document) for BadRequestError and AuthError since resending is pointless/masks bugs. For ContentPolicyError, do not automatically forward the same sensitive content to a different vendor — treat as a distinct failure mode requiring explicit policy sign-off before any fallback vendor call is made.

report_1.evidence: Path A directly violates Guardrail 3 ("fail closed... do not gate fail-open/fail-closed behavior on an 'is this production' check"). ... Path C also violates Guardrail 3 directly: on any redis error, the limiter fails open... Fix: invert both defaults. Fail closed unless an explicit, narrowly-scoped local-dev override is set (not "not equal to production"), and fail closed on RedisError

report_2.evidence: This is reasonable for local/dev/staging *only if* STAGE is reliably set to "production" in the real prod environment — a typo or missing env var in prod deploy config... silently disables rate limiting in production with no error or log.

report_1.evidence: log the pseudonymized ID, drop or hash the document text, and allowlist metadata keys: ... ALLOWED_METADATA_KEYS = {"document_type", "vendor", "status"} ... "input_text_hash": hashlib.sha256(record.input_text.encode()).hexdigest()

report_2.evidence: never log input_text verbatim (log a length/hash/redacted excerpt instead, or omit entirely and rely on a separate access-controlled store if the raw text must be retained for audit), and audit/allow-list what metadata is permitted to contain before logging it.
Fail loudly if PII_SALT is not set (raise at import/startup time, or raise inside pseudonymize rather than defaulting to "") rather than silently falling back to no salt.
